app.title: MindStep
app.subtitle: Early Dyslexia Detection Platform
app.totalTime: Total time: ~10-15 minutes • 100% confidential • Suitable for ages 5-10

welcome.readingDesc: Read a short story while we track your eye movements (2-3 min)
welcome.writingDesc: Write a few simple words using your mouse or touchscreen (2-3 min)
welcome.chatDesc: Have a friendly conversation with our AI assistant (3-5 min)
welcome.resultsDesc: Receive detailed insights and personalized recommendations

processing.title: Analyzing Your Results...
processing.subtitle: Our AI is processing your assessment data

reading.modalDesc: We'll track your eye movements while you read a short story. This helps us understand your reading patterns.
reading.modalInstructions: First, you'll calibrate the eye tracker by clicking on dots that appear on the screen. Then, read the story at your normal pace.
reading.completeDesc: Reading assessment complete. Analyzing your eye movements...
reading.readingText: Once upon a time, there was a clever fox who lived in a beautiful forest. The fox loved to explore and find new adventures every day. One sunny morning, the fox discovered a sparkling stream with crystal clear water. Many colorful fish swam happily in the stream. The fox made friends with a wise old owl who lived in a tall oak tree. Together, they explored the magical forest and helped other animals. They found hidden treasures and solved interesting puzzles. The fox learned that friendship and kindness are the most valuable treasures of all.

writing.modalDesc: We'll ask you to write a few simple words. This helps us analyze your handwriting patterns.
writing.modalInstructions: Use your mouse or finger (on touchscreen) to write on the canvas. Take your time and write naturally.
writing.analyzingDesc: Using AI to analyze your handwriting patterns.
writing.completeDesc: Great handwriting! Moving to the next assessment...

chatbot.modalDesc: I'll ask you a few easy questions. There are no wrong answers — just do your best and have fun!
chatbot.modalInstructions: Listen to each question (you can press the speaker button to hear it again) and type your answer. This takes about 2 minutes!

questions.q1: Hi! Can you remember these 3 words? Cat, Ball, Tree. Say them back to me!
questions.q3: Nice! What color is the sky on a sunny day?
questions.q4: Let me tell you a short story! 'Emma had a red ball. She threw it to her dog Max. Max caught it and ran to the tree.' Who caught the ball?
questions.q5: You're doing great! Do you remember those 3 words from the beginning? What were they?

results.completedAll: You completed all the questions! Here's how you did:
results.memoryDesc: How well your child remembers information
results.memoryDetail: Tested with word recall (immediate & delayed). These skills are essential for learning to read — children must remember letter sounds and sight words.
results.attentionDesc: How well your child focuses on questions
results.attentionDetail: Tested with a general knowledge question requiring focused thinking. Attention difficulties can make reading feel exhausting.
results.comprehensionDesc: How well your child understands language
results.comprehensionDetail: Tested with rhyming (phonological awareness) and story comprehension. Both are strong predictors of reading ability.
results.lowRiskMsg: ✅ Your child performed well across all cognitive areas tested. Continue with regular reading activities and check back in 6 months.
results.moderateRiskMsg1: ⚠️ Some areas scored below the expected range. This doesn't mean there's a problem — many factors can affect performance.
results.moderateRiskMsg2: Recommended: Try the suggested exercises in the Exercise Hub, and consider a follow-up assessment in 3 months.
results.highRiskMsg1: 🔴 One or more areas scored significantly below average. This may indicate areas where your child could benefit from additional support.
results.highRiskMsg2: Recommended: Consult with an educational psychologist or learning specialist for a comprehensive evaluation. Use the Exercise Hub for targeted practice in the meantime.
results.testedMemoryDesc: Your child was asked to remember three words at the start and recall them later. Working memory is crucial for reading — children must hold letters and sounds in mind while decoding words.
results.testedPhonologicalDesc: A rhyming task tested awareness of word sounds. Difficulty with rhyming is one of the earliest indicators of dyslexia.
results.testedAttentionDesc: A simple factual question assessed whether your child was focused and engaged. Attention challenges can amplify reading difficulties.
results.testedStoryDesc: Listening to a short story and answering a question tested language processing. Comprehension difficulties may signal deeper decoding issues.
results.scoreNote: This chatbot assessment contributes 20% to the overall MindStep diagnosis score, combined with eye-tracking (45% + 10%) and handwriting analysis (25%).

complete.title: All Done — Great Job!
complete.subtitle: Your answers have been recorded. Moving to final results...

dashboard.title: Assessment Results
dashboard.subtitle: Comprehensive Dyslexia Screening Report
dashboard.exerciseTip: Tip: Regular practice with these exercises can help improve reading and writing skills. We recommend 10-15 minutes of practice 3-4 times per week for best results.

pdf.title: MindStep — Dyslexia Screening Report
pdf.disclaimer: This is a screening tool, not a clinical diagnosis. Please consult a qualified professional for comprehensive evaluation.
